liveComparison.command: npm install --no-save tailwindcss@4.3.0 @tailwindcss/cli@4.3.0 && npx tailwindcss -i input.css -o tailwind.css
liveComparison.fixtureBinaryFreshnessPolicy: DX_STYLE_FIXTURE_CSS_BIN must be newer than related-crates/style/src Rust source files and the active official fixture matrix unless DX_STYLE_ALLOW_STALE_FIXTURE_BIN=1 is set for local debugging.
liveComparison.receiptArtifactPolicy: The live runner writes the full JSON receipt before setting a nonzero exit code, so failed comparisons still produce lane handoff artifacts.
liveComparison.blockedReceiptPolicy: Infrastructure failures that prevent class comparison emit blocked receipts with fullTailwindParity false instead of being mixed into Tailwind-only classname gaps.
liveComparison.candidateInventoryCoveragePolicy: The live runner reports how many matrix classes are present in the official source-test candidate inventory, how many are docs/release canaries outside that inventory, and any duplicate matrix classes without reclassifying CSS comparison failures.
liveComparison.inputFingerprintPolicy: Live receipts include stable SHA-256 fingerprints for the matrix class list, matrix comparison contract, official candidate inventory, and official fixture snapshot index so handoffs can detect stale or mixed inputs.
liveComparison.matrixIntegrityPolicy: Live comparison receipts report matrix integrity and block duplicate class entries before comparison so exact, known-different, and Tailwind-only gap counts cannot be inflated by duplicate canaries.
liveComparison.classificationSummaryPolicy: Live receipts summarize exact-fragment, known-different, and Tailwind-only-gap classifications for the governed matrix before any full-parity claim; known-different entries, when present, remain covered but are not exact output matches.
liveComparison.comparisonResultSummaryPolicy: Live comparison receipts summarize actual pass/fail results by comparison mode and owner lane so exact, known-different, and Tailwind-only failures can be handed to the right worker without reclassifying matrix policy.
liveComparison.evidenceQualityPolicy: Live receipts mark whether a run is canonical live comparison evidence; stale allowed fixture binaries, provided Tailwind CSS fixtures, and blocked runs are local diagnostics and must not be used for full Tailwind parity claims.
liveComparison.tailwindCssFixturePolicy: Only Lane 7 tests may pass prebuilt Tailwind CSS fixtures; normal governed comparison must invoke the temporary Tailwind CLI install path.

sourceScannerCanaries.scope: Tailwind v4.3 plain-text source detection canaries for complete static class strings; not full source graph or parser parity

canaries.description: Static object-map strings used by React/Vue style prop mapping are scanned as complete class strings.
Static quoted class strings in arrays, object maps, and helper calls are scanned when they are safe complete class tokens.
Arbitrary values and arbitrary properties in static strings are scanner candidates.
Dynamic interpolated fragments are not treated as generated class tokens.
Dynamic class fragments that leak into scanned inventories are reported as source scanner boundary diagnostics instead of missing utility parity.
Rejected static source-scan candidates are receipted with reason, token, and line and column diagnostics instead of disappearing silently.
CSS participates through @source inline(...) and @source not inline(...) rather than default CSS file scanning.

knownDifferences: dynamic class fragments are never generated as CSS; dx-style reports them as source scanner boundary diagnostics when they leak into scanned inventories
dx-style filters plain-text candidates through a Tailwind-like utility prefix allowlist before unsupported-class diagnostics to avoid prose false positives
full Tailwind source graph traversal and package boundary semantics remain unproven

utilityDocsCoverage.scope: representative Tailwind docs-table fixture coverage; not full value/modifier parity

missingOrUnproven: source graph file IO and glob expansion
multiple @slot blocks and nested selector-list ordering
cross-file @reference outside the public DX style build path
full Tailwind color namespace resolution inside --alpha()
full Tailwind arbitrary value grammar inside --spacing()

classes.differenceNote: Tailwind v4.3 emits default palette colors through theme variables; dx-style emits resolved sRGB for the older core palettes.
Tailwind v4.3 emits default palette colors through theme variables; dx-style emits resolved sRGB for the older core palettes.
Tailwind v4.3 emits default palette colors through theme variables; dx-style emits resolved sRGB for the older core palettes.
Tailwind v4.3 documents marker as an inheritable pseudo-element variant using both direct and descendant marker selectors.
Tailwind v4.3 emits selection as both direct and descendant selection selectors so parent-level selection styles cascade.
Tailwind v4.3 documents file as ::file-selector-button; dx-style now emits the standard selector without the legacy WebKit upload-button fallback in core output.
Tailwind v4.3 documents the direct child variant as :is(& > *), preserving selector specificity while targeting direct children.
Tailwind v4.3 documents the descendant child variant as :is(& *), preserving selector specificity while targeting all descendants.
Tailwind v4.3 emits both a :not(*:hover) selector branch and a no-hover fallback media branch; dx-style now matches those selector and media fragments.
dx-style now matches Tailwind v4.3's group :is/:where selector wrapper for this state variant.
dx-style matches Tailwind v4.3's group :is/:where selector wrapper for focus state variants.
dx-style matches Tailwind v4.3's named group selector wrapper for focus state variants.
dx-style applies Tailwind's group-* selector grammar to arbitrary data attribute variants with named groups.
dx-style now matches Tailwind v4.3 spacing variable emission while matching the peer data selector wrapper.
dx-style now matches Tailwind v4.3's peer :is/:where selector wrapper for this state variant.
dx-style now matches Tailwind v4.3's named peer :is/:where selector wrapper for this state variant.
Tailwind v4.3 emits percentage opacity; dx-style currently emits equivalent decimal opacity while matching the named peer selector wrapper.
Tailwind v4.3 emits nested arbitrary selector CSS; dx-style emits the equivalent flattened selector. Both outputs now preserve the covered spacing-token calc serialization for mt-4.
Tailwind v4.3 emits a nested :not(*:is(...)) selector and percentage opacity; dx-style emits the equivalent flattened selector.
Tailwind v4.3 emits nested selector-list arbitrary variants; dx-style emits the equivalent flattened Cartesian selector branches.
Tailwind v4.3 emits nested arbitrary group selector CSS; dx-style emits the equivalent flattened selector.
Tailwind v4.3 allows & placement inside arbitrary group selectors; dx-style emits the equivalent flattened selector.
Tailwind v4.3 wraps arbitrary group selector lists that place & in multiple branches with an inner :is(...); dx-style emits the equivalent flattened selector.
Tailwind v4.3 keeps commas inside :is(...) as selector-function grammar rather than a top-level list; dx-style preserves that distinction.
Tailwind v4.3 emits nested named arbitrary group selector CSS; dx-style emits the equivalent flattened selector.
Tailwind v4.3 emits nested arbitrary peer selector CSS; dx-style emits the equivalent flattened selector.
Tailwind v4.3 wraps arbitrary peer selector lists that place & in multiple branches with an inner :is(...); dx-style emits the equivalent flattened selector.
Tailwind v4.3 allows & placement inside arbitrary peer selectors; dx-style emits the equivalent flattened selector.
Tailwind v4.3 emits nested peer selector wrappers for stacked peer variants; dx-style emits the equivalent flattened selector.
Tailwind v4.3 emits nested arbitrary group plus arbitrary selector CSS and percentage opacity; dx-style emits the equivalent flattened selector.
Tailwind v4.3 preserves the named container before the negated arbitrary container query; dx-style matches that at-rule ordering.
